MEDIA AND PUBLIC REPORTED FLASH FLOODING AT BISCAYNE BLVD AND 23RD STREET. VIDEO SHOWS WATER RISING ABOVE THE BOTTOM LINE OF SEVERAL BUILDING DOORS. ALSO, WATER IS COVERING THE WHEELS OF CARS, AND RISING TO KNEE-HIGH LEVEL. BETWEEN 3 TO 4 INCHES OF RAIN HAVE FALLEN IN THE WARNED AREA.

AN NWS EMPLOYEE OBSERVED A FAIR WEATHER WATERSPOUT LOCATED APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES EAST OF OCEAN REEF. THE WATERSPOUT WAS CHARACTERIZED BY A ROPE-LIKE APPEARANCE. IT DEVELOPED AT APPROXIMATELY 6:50 AM EDT AND PERSISTED FOR APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES PRIOR TO DISSIPATING.

*** 1 FATAL *** CORRECTS PREVIOUS MARINE TSTM WIND REPORT FROM 2 E WILTON MANORS. BROADCAST MEDIA, WSVN, REPORTED THAT A KITESURFER IS PRONOUNCED DEAD DUE TO SIGNIFICANT INJURIES SUSTAINED AFTER BEING BLOWN OFF COURSE AT FORT LAUDERDALE BEACH. OFFICIALS SAID THE MAN WAS KITESURFING WHEN THE WIND SUDDENLY PICKED UP AND LAUNCHED HIM INTO A NEARBY BUILDING. THERE WERE STRONG, GUSTY THUNDERSTORMS AND WINDS IN THE AREA AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT. FORT LAUDERDALE FIRE RESCUE RUSHED THE MAN TO BROWARD HEALTH MEDICAL CENTER, WHERE HE WAS LATER PRONOUNCED DEAD DUE TO INJURIES SUSTAINED FROM THE INCIDENT.
